accurate.why: Key claims match bill text: NIH fields expanded, Centers of Excellence period extended, reauthorization through FY2029, appropriation figures all consistent with bill text.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; appropriation figures and statutory details are supported by the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as reauthorizing and expanding autism research and programs under the PHSA.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Details specific amendments to PHSA sections including expanded research fields, Centers of Excellence period, and reauthorization dates.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes conditions such as NIH consulting with federal agencies and budget estimates being tied to strategic plan.

effective_dates.why: Notes reauthorization through September 30, 2029 and NIH budget estimates for FY2026-2029.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers NIH activities, program reauthorizations, IACC reforms, reporting requirements, appropriations, and communication tools section.

coherent.why: Well-organized by bill section with clear structure.

concise.why: Bold headers and granular amendment-level details make this more verbose than necessary for a summary; some repetition of information across sections.

correct_entities.why: NIH, PHSA, HHS, Interagency Autism Coordinating Committee all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: Appropriation amounts $28,100,000, $56,344,000, $306,000,000 and FY2025-2029 authorization period match bill.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
